2604.26951.title: Turning the TIDE: Cross-Architecture Distillation for Diffusion Large Language Models
2604.26951.paper_summary: Diffusion large language models (dLLMs) offer parallel decoding and bidirectional context, but state-of-the-art dLLMs require billions of parameters for competitive performance. While existing distillation methods for dLLMs reduce inference steps within a single architecture, none address cross-architecture knowledge transfer, in which the teacher and student differ in architecture, attention mechanism, and tokenizer. We present TIDE, the first framework for cross-architecture dLLM distillation, comprising three modular components: (1) TIDAL, which jointly modulates distillation strength across training progress and diffusion timestep to account for the teacher's noise-dependent reliability; (2) CompDemo, which enriches the teacher's context via complementary mask splitting to improve predictions under heavy masking; and (3) Reverse CALM, a cross-tokenizer objective that inverts chunk-level likelihood matching, yielding bounded gradients and dual-end noise filtering. Distilling 8B dense and 16B MoE teachers into a 0.6B student via two heterogeneous pipelines outperforms the baseline by an average of 1.53 points across eight benchmarks, yielding notable gains in code generation, where HumanEval scores reach 48.78 compared to 32.3 for the AR baseline.
2604.26951.paper_summary_zh: 擴散大型語言模型（dLLMs）提供平行解碼和雙向上下文，但最先進的 dLLMs 需要數十億的參數以達到競爭性能。雖然現有的 dLLMs 蒸餾方法在單一架構內減少推理步驟，但沒有一種方法能解決跨架構知識轉移的問題，其中教師和學生在架構、注意力機制和標記器上有所不同。我們提出了 TIDE，第一個跨架構 dLLM 蒸餾框架，包含三個模組化組件：（1）TIDAL，該組件在訓練進展和擴散時間步驟中共同調節蒸餾強度，以考慮教師的噪聲依賴可靠性；（2）CompDemo，通過互補的掩碼分割來豐富教師的上下文，以改善在重度掩碼下的預測；以及（3）Reverse CALM，一個跨標記器的目標，反轉塊級似然匹配，產生有界梯度和雙端噪聲過濾。通過兩個異質管道將 8B 密集和 16B MoE 教師蒸餾為 0.6B 學生，在八個基準測試中平均超越基準 1.53 分，在代碼生成方面獲得顯著增益，其中 HumanEval 分數達到 48.78，相較於 AR 基準的 32.3。
2604.26951.author: Gongbo Zhang et.al.
2604.26951.authors: Gongbo Zhang, Wen Wang, Ye Tian, Li Yuan
